name: Hunyuan3D 2.1 Shape (Full)
description: Full-size Hunyuan3D-2.1 (3.3B params, fp16 ckpt). High-fidelity image-to-mesh geometry with an optional PBR texture pass (shape → paint, run sequentially). The texture pass cleans the mesh (Regular / Isotropic / BPT neural), paints albedo + packed metallic-roughness, reconciles UV-seam color jumps, and writes an optional QA sheet. Shape needs ~10 GB VRAM; textures run in ~13 GB with the reduced-VRAM path (~20 GB full GPU), plus a C++/CUDA build toolchain.
author: elevatormusic

params_schema.label: Quality
params_schema.tooltip: Number of diffusion steps. More steps = better quality but slower.

params_schema.label: Mesh Resolution
params_schema.tooltip: Octree resolution for mesh reconstruction. Higher = more detail, more VRAM, slower.

params_schema.label: Guidance Scale
params_schema.tooltip: Classifier-free guidance strength. Higher = closer to the input image.

params_schema.label: Decimate (for CAD/print)
params_schema.tooltip: Reduce polygon count on export. Helps downstream mesh-to-solid conversion (e.g. Fusion 360 Convert Mesh).

params_schema.label: Seed
params_schema.tooltip: Seed for reproducibility. Set to -1 for a random seed.

params_schema.label: Generate textures (PBR)
params_schema.tooltip: Paint PBR textures after the shape. Runs in ~13 GB VRAM on the reduced path (~20 GB full GPU); Auto fits it to your card. Needs a big first-run download and a C++/CUDA build toolchain. Textures are ignored by CAD/STEP export.

params_schema.label: Texture view resolution
params_schema.tooltip: Per-view render resolution for texture generation. Higher = sharper, more VRAM/time.

params_schema.label: Texture views
params_schema.tooltip: Number of camera views painted and baked. More views = better coverage, slower.

params_schema.label: Texture memory
params_schema.tooltip: How much VRAM the texture pass may use, at identical quality. Auto measures free VRAM and picks the full-GPU path (~20 GB) when it fits, else the reduced-VRAM path. Reduced runs the same quality with components staged between CPU and GPU (~13 GB, ~5% slower). 768 view resolution needs ~+14 GB (turn on Use shared GPU memory); each view above 6 adds ~+0.7 GB.

params_schema.label: Use shared GPU memory
params_schema.tooltip: Lets a texture run exceed your VRAM by paging to system RAM over PCIe — needed only for very high settings (e.g. 768 view resolution) on smaller cards. Much slower (tens of minutes) and needs a large Windows page file. Leave Off; Auto already fits normal runs to your card.

params_schema.label: Mesh cleanup
params_schema.tooltip: How the shape mesh is cleaned before texturing. Regular = quadric decimation, keeps one connected shell with clean UVs (recommended). Isotropic = uniform triangles but can shatter Hunyuan's non-watertight surfaces into many UV islands (auto-falls back to Regular). BPT = neural artist topology (first use downloads ~4 GB; falls back to Regular if unavailable).

params_schema.label: Bake normal map

options.label: Yes (bake detail from the dense mesh)

params_schema.tooltip: Bakes fine detail from the full-resolution mesh onto the clean base as a tangent-space normal map (tangents are computed and shipped in the file, so it renders correctly in any glTF viewer). Off by default — it adds a few seconds and helps most on detailed or hard-surface subjects. Only applies when textures are on.

params_schema.label: Fix texture seams
params_schema.tooltip: Reconcile UV-seam color jumps in the baked texture so island edges don't show hard color breaks. On by default; turn off for the raw bake. Only applies when textures are on.

params_schema.label: QA debug sheet
params_schema.tooltip: Optional diagnostic: writes a QA image (*_qa.png) beside each textured GLB - albedo, metallic, roughness, normal, UV layout and mesh/texture stats. Off by default; turn on when checking texture quality. Doesn't change the model. Only applies when textures are on.
